items.title: Rhoda Roberts held Country. Now Country will hold her forever
items.summary: Rhoda Roberts AO loved Country and its stories. An NITV special 'Forever Held in Country' pays tribute to her enduring connection to the lands, skies and waters of this continent.

items.title: Lea says she and her sister didn't talk for a year. She blames their age gap
items.summary: Sibling estrangement is common — and can arise from rivalry or differing life stages.

items.title: Outcry as Israel blocks cardinal from Jerusalem's Holy Sepulchre on Palm Sunday
items.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has since reversed the ban.

items.title: Who are the Houthis? Iran's 'powerful ally' putting 'significant' pressure on Trump
items.summary: The latest Houthi attack on Israel comes after years of back-and-forth attacks between the group and the US in the Red Sea.

items.title: A fuel excise cut is on its way, but is it a big 'mistake' for Australian households?
items.summary: Economists warn that a fuel excise cut could boost inflation — and make mortgage rate hikes more likely.

items.title: 'Evil man': A timeline of the seven-month manhunt for Dezi Freeman
items.summary: The search for alleged police shooter Dezi Freeman has been one of Australia's largest police operations in history.

items.title: The Epstein files have exposed the world's elite. But will justice be done?
items.summary: It's been two months since the latest tranche of Epstein files were released, and the impact on the world's elite continues to be felt.

items.title: For Australia's 'vanlifers', the fuel crisis is a headache — or a threat to survival
items.summary: For some, Australia's fuel crisis means a disrupted road trip. For others, it means losing the only roof over their head.

items.title: Fuel excise slashed, relief for truckies as Australia enters stage two of four-point plan
items.summary: The $2.55 billion measure will be in place for three months — as the government focuses on cost relief for Australians.

items.title: Who's profiting from your outrage?
items.summary: Intentionally triggering, offensive and outrageous videos about Australian migrant communities are proliferating online. But the videos aren't real — they're AI-generated — and the networks profiting off them are based overseas.

items.title: New government inquiry could turn up the heat on Australian gas exporters
items.summary: The war in the Middle East has Australian gas exporters on track for huge profits, prompting calls for an urgent 25 per cent tax on gas exports.

items.title: G7 ministers gather for economic talks amid demands for clarity on US war plans
items.summary: G7 ministers will meet on Monday as the US comes under pressure to clarify its war aims in the Middle East, and a path to ending hostilities.

items.title: Iran confirms death of top navy commander
items.summary: Israel had previously described Tangsiri as the man "directly responsible for blocking the Strait of Hormuz".

items.title: Netanyahu orders Israeli military to 'further expand' into southern Lebanon
items.summary: Benjamin Netanyahu said Israel was conducting a multi-front campaign against Iran and allied groups, including Hezbollah.

items.title: Pakistan offers to host peace talks as war in the Middle East enters second month
items.summary: The diplomatic push comes despite sharply opposing positions from the US, Israel and Iran on how the conflict should end.

items.title: Victoria and Tasmania offer free public transport — but not every state is on board
items.summary: Four Australian states have ruled out fare-free months.

items.title: Inside Lebanon's war zone where medics are targets too
items.summary: Lebanon's frontline medics risk their lives daily as war, displacement and attacks on healthcare push the system to breaking point. On Saturday, nine paramedics were killed in one day.

items.title: Locals and refugees are stuck in housing limbo in this city. It's fuelling a deep divide
items.summary: Ahead of Scottish elections, refugees and locals are struggling in Glasgow's housing crisis — and anti-immigration sentiment is rising.

items.title: 'Brazen crime': Lebanon condemns killing of three journalists in Israeli airstrikes
items.summary: Lebanon's information minister Paul Morcos deemed Israel's actions to be "war crimes".

items.title: Millions of people protest across the US in anti-Trump 'No Kings' rallies
items.summary: The No Kings movement launched last year on Trump's birthday and drew an estimated four to six million people across the US.

items.title: With Hormuz choked, how does the world reroute — and what does that mean for Australia?
items.summary: The closure of the narrow channel has sparked global fuel price spikes, and governments have been seeking alternatives to the route.

items.title: Is the war in the Middle East Australia's COVID 2.0 moment?
items.summary: Some have compared the ramifications of the war in the Middle East to those of the COVID-19 pandemic, but are they similar crises?

items.title: Albanese has announced new fuel powers. What are they and why do they matter?
items.summary: With fuel prices surging and ships cancelling, the government is stepping in to guarantee supply — but stopped short of rationing.

items.title: Yemen's Houthis launch first missile at Israel, have ability to disrupt another shipping route
items.summary: Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis may target a key shipping choke point that controls sea traffic towards the Suez Canal.
